message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should return an error when damages contain more entries of a type than the policy covers"
Expected behavior: throw error when damages have more entries of a type than insured items
Current error: "expected [Function] to throw an error" — function returns normally
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Read the implementation file first. Then add validation in the claim branch: count damages per itemType, count policy items per type, and throw if any damage type count exceeds policy item count.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll start by reading both the test file and the implementation file to understand the current state.
Now let me run the tests to see the current failing test and confirm the error.

content.text:
I can see the failing test. The test expects an error when damages contain more entries of a type than the policy covers. In the test case, the policy has 1 sword but the claim has 2 sword damages. The minimal fix is to add validation in the claim branch that counts damages per itemType and compares against policy items per type.

Let me implement the minimal validation.

content.text:
All 36 tests pass (8 remaining as todo).

**Green Phase Complete:**

**Implementation**: Added validation in the claim branch at `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-3/src/claim-office.ts` (lines 67-78). The code counts damages per `itemType`, counts policy items per `type`, and throws an error if any damage type count exceeds the corresponding policy item count.

**Result**: All 36 tests pass (8 todo).

**Approach**: This is minimal -- it only adds the validation the test demands. It counts occurrences using simple loops and a plain record, then throws when the condition is violated. No other logic was changed or optimized.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
